Steps:
Open the Marketplace

Locate the Alarm Management System category
Expected Result:
Category is visible

Only admin users can see and access CMS configuration options

Steps: Open Alarm Management System category
Expected Result:
All CMS options are displayed: Evalink Talos, Amwin, Lisa, Immix, Alice, Other (DC09-compatible)

Each CMS displays current state: Configured / Not configured

Integration type is clearly indicated (DC09 or API)

Steps:
Ensure no CMS is configured

Open Marketplace → Alarm Management System
Expected Result: All CMS entries are visible even if not configured

Steps:
Select any CMS entry

Open the overview
Expected Result:
Supported alarm protocol

Scope of integration

Required configuration inputs

Steps: Open Marketplace → Alarm Management System
Expected Result:
CMS entries are visible

CMS configuration options are not accessible

Steps:
Configure a CMS entry

Return to the Alarm Management System category
Expected Result:
The configured CMS state is updated to Configured

Other CMS entries remain Not configured

PRE CONDITIONS: User is logged in as Admin
Steps:
Open Marketplace

Navigate to Alarm Management System category

Select Evalink Talos
Expected Result:
Evalink Talos is selectable

Configuration form opens successfully

Steps:
Open Evalink Talos configuration form

Leave Company ID and/or API Token empty

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
System prevents saving

Clear error messages are displayed indicating required fields

Steps:
Open Evalink Talos configuration form

Focus on API Token field
Expected Result:
Inline guidance is displayed explaining:

Where to generate the API token in Talos

Required permission scope

Steps:
Enter invalid or improperly formatted Company ID or API Token

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
System validates format before saving

Invalid credentials cannot be saved

Clear, actionable error messages are displayed

Steps:
Enter valid Company ID and API Token

Save the configuration
Expected Result:
Evalink Talos is marked as Configured in Marketplace

Credentials are stored securely

API Token is not displayed in plain text after saving

Steps:
Enter valid Company ID and API Token

Save configuration

Check system logs or internal handling
Expected Result: System combines Company ID and API Token internally using the required format

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: DC09 Account ID Field Visible on Site Creation
PRE CONDITIONS: User is logged in as Admin
Steps:
Open the Create Site form

Locate the DC09 Account ID field
Expected Result:
The field is visible

Field is required for sites forwarding alarms to a DC09-based CMS

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: DC09 Account ID Field Visible and Editable on Site Edit
PRE CONDITIONS: User is logged in as Admin
Steps:
Open an existing site in edit mode

Locate the DC09 Account ID field
Expected Result: Field is visible and editable

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Prevent Saving Site Without DC09 Account ID
PRE CONDITIONS:
A DC09-based CMS is configured at tenant level

Site is intended to forward alarms
Steps: Attempt to save a site without entering DC09 Account ID
Expected Result:
System prevents saving

Clear error message displayed indicating DC09 Account ID is required

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: DC09 Account ID Must Be Unique Per CMS
Steps:
Assign a DC09 Account ID to a site

Attempt to assign the same ID to another site targeting the same CMS
Expected Result:
System prevents duplicate assignment

Clear guidance displayed

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Guidance for Obtaining DC09 Account ID
Steps: Focus on DC09 Account ID field during site creation/edit
Expected Result: Guidance clearly explains where to obtain the DC09 Account ID from the CMS

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Immediate Effect of DC09 Account ID Changes
Steps:
Change the DC09 Account ID for a site

Trigger alarms for that site
Expected Result:
Changes take effect immediately

Alarms are routed according to the new DC09 Account ID

PRE CONDITIONS: User is logged in as Admin
Steps:
Open Site Creation form

Open Site Edit form
Expected Result:
Encryption Key field is visible in both Site Creation and Site Edit

Field is marked as optional

Steps:
Create or edit a site without entering an Encryption Key

Save the site
Expected Result:
Site is saved successfully

DC09 alarms are sent unencrypted

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Save Site With Valid Encryption Key
Steps:
Enter a valid Encryption Key

Save the site

Trigger a DC09 alarm for the site
Expected Result:
Site is saved successfully

DC09 alarm messages are sent using encrypted communication

Steps:
Enter an invalid or incorrectly formatted Encryption Key

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
System validates the Encryption Key format before saving

Saving is blocked

Clear and actionable error message is displayed

Steps:
Save a site with a valid Encryption Key

Reopen the site configuration
Expected Result:
Encryption Key is not displayed in plain text

Key is stored securely

Steps: View the Encryption Key field help or inline guidance
Expected Result:
Clear guidance explains:

When encryption is required

How encryption affects alarm transmission and testing

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Immediate Effect of Encryption Key Changes
Steps:
Update the Encryption Key for an existing site

Trigger subsequent DC09 alarms
Expected Result:
Changes take effect immediately

Subsequent alarms follow the updated encryption setting

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Real-Time Validation on CMS Configuration Inputs
PRE CONDITIONS: User is logged in as Admin
Steps:
Open CMS configuration form

Enter invalid data in any required field
Expected Result:
Real-time validation is triggered

Validation error is displayed immediately

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Block Saving CMS Configuration with Missing Required Fields
Steps:
Leave one or more required CMS fields empty

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
System blocks saving

Clear error message indicates the missing field(s)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Block Saving CMS Configuration with Invalid IP or Port Format
Steps:
Enter an invalid IP address or port value

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
System blocks saving

Validation error clearly identifies the invalid field and required correction

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Block Saving CMS Configuration with Missing CMS-Specific Mandatory Fields
Steps:
Open configuration for a CMS that requires specific mandatory fields

Leave those fields empty

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
Saving is blocked

Error message indicates the missing CMS-specific fields

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Block Duplicate or Contradictory CMS Configurations
Steps:
Configure a CMS

Attempt to create a duplicate or conflicting CMS configuration
Expected Result:
System detects the duplicate or contradiction

Saving is blocked with a clear validation message

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Block Saving Site Without DC09 Account ID
PRE CONDITIONS:
DC09 CMS is configured at tenant level

Site is intended to forward alarms
Steps: Attempt to save site without DC09 Account ID
Expected Result:
Saving is blocked

Error message clearly indicates DC09 Account ID is required

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Block Saving Site When Required CMS Configuration Is Missing
Steps:
Attempt to save a site configured to forward alarms

No CMS is configured at tenant level
Expected Result:
System blocks saving

Error message indicates missing tenant-level CMS configuration

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Validation Errors Must Be Resolved Before Saving
Steps:
Trigger multiple validation errors

Attempt to save
Expected Result:
System prevents saving

All validation errors must be resolved before save is allowed

Steps: Trigger a validation error
Expected Result:
Error message clearly states:

Which field is invalid

What correction is required

Steps: Save CMS and site configuration successfully
Expected Result:
Confirmation view is displayed

Summary includes:

Configured CMS

Tenant-level settings

Site-level DC09 account mapping

Encryption status

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Display DC09 Alarm Forward Log Section
PRE CONDITIONS:
User is logged in as Admin

Device belongs to a site that forwards alarms using DC09
Steps: Open Device Dashboard
Expected Result: DC09 Alarm Forward Log section is visible on the dashboard

Steps: Open dashboard for a device not associated with DC09 forwarding
Expected Result: DC09 Alarm Forward Log section is hidden or not displayed

Steps: Open DC09 Alarm Forward Log section
Expected Result:
Each log entry displays the following fields:

Time

Alarm Code

DC09 Code

State

Account

Response

Encrypted

IP Address

Port

Area

Zone

Ack Time

Camera

Attachment

Steps: Attempt to edit, replay, or modify a log entry
Expected Result:
No edit or replay actions are available

Log entries are read-only

Steps: Trigger a valid alarm from a DC09-enabled device
Expected Result:
A new log entry is created

Response indicates successful forwarding

Encrypted field reflects correct encryption status

Steps: Trigger an alarm with invalid CMS configuration or network issue
Expected Result:
Failed transmission is logged

Response field clearly indicates failure or error reason

Steps: Trigger an alarm
Expected Result: Encrypted field shows correct value (e.g. Yes / No)

Steps: Generate multiple alarm events
Expected Result: Log entries are ordered by most recent first

Steps:
Trigger an alarm

Observe DC09 Alarm Forward Log section
Expected Result: Log entry appears in near real time without manual page refresh (or with minimal delay)

Steps: Trigger an alarm for a device linked to a specific DC09 account
Expected Result: Account, IP Address, Port, Area, and Zone fields display correct values

Steps: Trigger an alarm with camera snapshot or attachment
Expected Result:
Camera and Attachment fields display correct information

If no attachment exists, fields are empty or marked as N/A
